summary: The full dichotomy of $k$-Coloring on $H$-free graphs remains open for every $k\geq 3$. The source paper settled the analogous List-$k$-Coloring dichotomy for $k>4$ but explicitly noted that for plain $k$-Coloring (without lists) no value of $k\geq 3$ is known for which the polynomial/NP-hard boundary is fully characterised. For $k=3$, open cases such as $H=P_8$ and $H=2P_4$ persist; for $k=4$, a 2025 paper narrows the complexity gap for $(P_t,C_3)$-free graphs (showing NP-hardness for $t\in\{19,20,21\}$) but a complete characterisation remains elusive. No follow-up paper resolves the conjecture for any single value of $k$.

since_posted.title: Coloring Problem in $(P_t,C_3)$-free Graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves NP-completeness of 4-Coloring on $(P_t,C_3)$-free graphs for $t\in\{19,20,21\}$, narrowing the complexity gap for 4-Coloring in this two-forbidden-subgraph family but leaving the full $k$-Coloring dichotomy open.

search_queries: k-Coloring H-free graphs dichotomy polynomial NP-hard characterization
"k-coloring" "H-free" dichotomy complexity 3-coloring 4-coloring 2024 2025 polynomial NP-hard forbidden induced subgraph
notes: The conjecture is distinct from the List-k-Coloring dichotomy, which was settled by the source paper itself for k>4. For plain k-Coloring, the smallest open cases for k=3 are H=P_8 and H=2P_4; for k=4 the complexity of Pt-free graphs for 7<=t<=18 is unknown. No paper found that resolves the dichotomy for any single k>=3.
